arts.ttl: Donyell Malen sends new Aston Villa team-mates inspired Champions League message
arts.sum: Aston Villa completed the signing of forward Donyell Malen from Borussia Dortmund for £21million less than eight months after he helped the German club reach the Champions League final

arts.ttl: Maatsen texts, Emery's influence, special tattoos - Donyell Malen interview after Aston Villa transfer - Birmingham Live
arts.sum: "In January, I spoke to my agent and from there spoke to them and had a really good feeling with the plan and what they are trying to do with the club," Malen said about his move to Villa. I think as a player it is a good thing to have, being able to play different positions.

arts.ttl: Youri Tielemans now the pacemaker at heart of Aston Villa’s resurgence
arts.sum: Former Leicester midfielder has proved sceptics wrong with displays central to the transformation of Unai Emery’s side Some things simply meet expectations, others catch us cold, take us by pleasant surprise. Aston Villa have made a habit of doing the latter over the past couple of years. Take the supporters who ended up slurping a pre‑match pint and talking double pivots with the Prince of Wales in the Wetherspoon’s at Birmingham New Street in the hours before they travelled to the win at Everton last week, after the royal Villa fan attended an inaugural critical‑care conference in the city. Nobody envisaged Unai Emery transforming Villa in double-quick time, to the point where a victory in Monaco on Tuesday will put them on the verge of qualifying for the Champions League last 16 in their first season back on this stage for 41 years. There were plenty of sceptics when the Villa manager plucked Morgan Rogers out of the Championship 12 months ago but he has proved a revelation, one of the Premier League’s best performers this season, repeatedly leaving opponents in a tailspin. And few predicted Youri Tielemans would return to the peak of his powers after signing on a free 18 months ago. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: Aston Villa turn down second bid from West Ham for Jhon Durán
arts.sum: Aston Villa unhappy with timing of the offer £100m-valued striker signed new deal in October Aston Villa have rejected a second bid from West Ham for Jhon Durán out of hand, with Villa adamant the striker, who they value at about £100m, is not for sale. Duran signed a new contract until 2030 in October last year. Villa are thought to be livid at the timing of the offer from West Ham, who are not expected to return with another bid after Villa made their position clear on Durán. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: Aston Villa reject West Ham bid for forward Jhon Duran
arts.sum: Duran has scored 12 times in 27 appearances for Villa this season but has only started seven matches.

arts.ttl: The truth behind £57m Jhon Duran bid as Aston Villa transfer stance revealed
arts.sum: It's thought that the offer submitted by the Hammers was lower than the £57m quoted and Villa are unhappy by the timing of the news, a day before their penultimate Champions League league phase clash against AS Monaco. Duran had replaced Watkins as Villa's starting striker at the start of December before he was sent off at Newcastle on Boxing Day, which brought a three-match ban.

arts.ttl: Aston Villa reject £57m bid for Jhon Duran from West Ham - with PSG also keen on Colombia striker
arts.sum: Duran is Villa's top scorer this season with 12 goals though he may again be on the bench for Tuesday's Champions League match in Monaco.

arts.ttl: Aston Villa table €25m offer for La Liga star
arts.sum: Premier League outfit Aston Villa have moved forward with an opening offer for the signature of Sevilla star Loïc Badé. That’s according to Spanish outlet El Chiringuito TV, who have…

arts.ttl: Team News: Monaco vs. Aston Villa injury, suspension list, predicted XIs
arts.sum: Sports Mole rounds up all of the latest injury and suspension news ahead of Tuesday's Champions League clash between Monaco and Aston Villa.

arts.ttl: Transfer news and rumours today: Diego Carlos to Fenerbahce, Luis Guilherme to Al-Hilal
arts.sum: Sports Mole rounds up the latest transfer news and rumours, including Diego Carlos to Fenerbahce and Luis Guilherme's possible West Ham United exit.
